title: put() with ?key= uses merge-patch + 404→create fallthrough for atomic update-or-create
description: For ?key= puts: JSON merge patch preserves all keys OTHER than the target key, which is what the user expects when updating a single field in a multi-key Secret. Server-side apply for per-key writes would require SSA field manager semantics that conflict when mixing keyed and unkeyed paths. Merge patch is simpler and correct here. A contributor might suggest 'just use SSA everywhere' — doing so for per-key writes would cause conflicts between keyed and unkeyed field managers on the same Secret.
